title: Simulation Scenarios
description: Schema for OntoUML simulation scenario definitions. Each scenario defines an Alloy template and corresponding natural-language template and their parameters, to be replaced render time.

version.description: Schema version (1.1.0: camelCase JSON property names).

conflictGroups.description: Catalogue with groups of mutually exclusive scenarios. Keys are the ids referenced from each scenario's `conflictGroups` array. Two scenarios are mutually exclusive iff their `conflictGroups` arrays share a key defined here.

Scenario.description: A single user-selectable scenario with its own Alloy and natural-language templates.

category.description: Catalogue tag used only to group scenarios in the GUI picker

conflictGroups.description: Ids of mutual-exclusion groups this scenario belongs to; each must be a key of the top-level `conflictGroups` catalogue. Required (empty array allowed) so authors must explicitly consider whether the scenario needs any.

reference.description: Pointer to source material if applicable. Not consumed by the runtime.

displayDescription.description: Hand-authored sentence shown in the GUI's scenario description box, following Sales (2014)'s bracketed-slot convention (`[at least / at most / exactly]`, `[n]`, `[Class]`). Kept separate from `naturalLanguageTemplate` for documentation, and because the heavily factored scenarios are compex to derive a readable preview from.
examples: I want to see a story composed [at least / at most / exactly] of [n] consecutive worlds.

parameters.description: The scenario's parameters. Every `<<id>>` placeholder in the templates must be backed by a top-level parameter or a sub-parameter of the currently-selected enum choice.

alloyTemplate.description: Alloy constraint template with <<id>> expressions. The root may be either an explicit formula (e.g. 'all w: World | ...') or a placeholder whose selected enum choice expands to a full formula (e.g. '<<world_quantifier>>').

naturalLanguageTemplate.description: Natural-language template with <<id>> expressions to render according to argument choices.
examples: I want to see a story composed of at least <<n>> consecutive worlds.

parameterConstraints.description: Constraints on parameter-combinations within a scenario, e.g. disallowing two `class_ref` parameters to point to the same class. Required (empty array allowed) so authors must explicitly consider whether the scenario needs any.

Parameter.description: A placeholder for a user-supplied value substituted into `alloyTemplate` and `naturalLanguageTemplate`. Type-specific fields and constraints (e.g. `min`/`max`/`default` only on `int`, `choices` required on `enum`) are documented per-field and enforced by the Kotlin loader, rather than in the schema.

id.description: Identifier used to reference this parameter (e.g. id `n` is referenced as `<<n>>`); must be unique within its scenario.

sourceClassParameter.description: (association parameters only) Id of the class_ref parameter playing the source-endpoint role; may equal `targetClassParam` for self-loop associations.

targetClassParameter.description: (association parameters only) Id of the class_ref parameter playing the target-endpoint role; may equal `sourceClassParam` for self-loop associations.

ParameterConstraint.description: Constraint on parameter-combinations within a scenario, e.g. disallowing two `class_ref` parameters to point to the same class.

type.description: `distinct` requires the listed parameters to resolve to pairwise different values.

params.description: List of parameters (ids) that need to resolve to different values. Each must match a parameter id on the enclosing scenario.

Guards.description: Guards to prevent the solver from returning vacuously-true instances. Defining sets that can't be empty etc.

worldScope.description: Minimum number of World atoms required to simulate this scenario.

NonEmptyClasses.description: Class_ref parameters of the scenario whose chosen class must have a non-empty extension at the given scope.

classes.description: Ids of class_ref parameters whose chosen class must be non-empty at the declared `scope`.

NonEmptyScope.description: Quantifier scope at which each class/relation must be non-empty. `per_world` emits `all w: World | some w.<X>` (use when the scenario universally quantifies over worlds and an empty world would let an inner universal hold vacuously); `some_world` emits `some w: World | some w.<X>` (use when the scenario only meaningfully refers to at least one world).

EnumChoice.description: Choice for an enum-type parameter. A choice may declare its own conditional sub-parameters.

id.description: Unique identifier for this choice within the parameter.

alloySnippet.description: Alloy fragment substituted wherever the parameter's `<<id>>` placeholder appears in `alloyTemplate`. May contain its own placeholders, resolved in subsequent passes.

naturalLanguageSnippet.description: Natural-language fragment substituted wherever the parameter's `<<id>>` placeholder appears in `naturalLanguageTemplate`. May contain its own placeholders.

parameters.description: Optional sub-parameters that only become active while this choice is selected. They share the scenario's flat namespace and must not collide with top-level parameter ids.

_TemplateFunctions.description: Documentation-only. Lists the template functions recognised inside `<<...>>` placeholders in Alloy template fields. Function arguments accept simple arithmetic on parameter names (n, n+1, n-1). Implementation lives in the Kotlin codebase.

world_vars(n).description: Comma-separated world variable names: 'w1, w2, ..., wn'.

world_chain(n).description: Predicate chaining world variables w1 through wn via `next`.
examples: world_chain(3) -> w2 in w1.next and w3 in w2.next

x_vars(n).description: Comma-separated element variable names: 'x1, x2, ..., xn'.

x_chain(n, association).description: Predicate chaining element variables x1 through xn via the given association parameter.
examples: x_chain(3, relatedTo) -> x2 in relatedTo[x1, w] and x3 in relatedTo[x2, w]

all_classes_at_least(n).description: Conjunction `#w.C1 >= n and #w.C2 >= n and ...` over every endurant class; must be used inside a scope binding `w` to a World.
examples: all w: World | <<all_classes_at_least(2)>> -> all w: World | #w.Person >= 2 and #w.Detective >= 2 and ...

all_associations_at_least(n).description: Conjunction `#w.r1 >= n and #w.r2 >= n and ...` over every association; must be used inside a scope binding `w` to a World.
examples: all w: World | <<all_associations_at_least(1)>> -> all w: World | #w.involvesdetective >= 1 and #w.manages >= 1 and ...

neighbors_nonempty(mc_alloy_name, k).description: Conjunction `#w.C1 >= 1 and #w.C2 >= 1 and ...` over every endurant class within `k` undirected hops (over associations and generalisations) of the main-concept class `mc_alloy_name`. Must be used inside a scope binding `w` to a World.
examples: all w: World | <<neighbors_nonempty(class_name, 2)>> -> all w: World | #w.Detective >= 1 and #w.Interrogation >= 1 and ...
